d7b_reasoning: The strategy combines volume anomaly detection (volume_zscore_24h > 2.0) with price breakout (close crosses_above sma_20) while filtering for early momentum (rsi_14 < 60). This logic is internally coherent: high volume confirms breakout strength while the RSI cap aims to avoid overbought entries. Exit conditions balance mean-reversion (volume_zscore_24h < -0.5 or price crosses_below sma_20) with profit-taking (return_168h > 0.1). The 240-bar max_hold provides reasonable downside protection. Theme alignment is strong as volume_zscore_24h appears in both entry and exit, directly addressing volume divergence dynamics. However, structural variant risk is elevated because the factor set {close, return_168h, rsi_14, sma_20, volume_zscore_24h} closely resembles multiple priors: entry 19 uses {close, macd_hist, return_1h, rsi_14, sma_20, volume_zscore_24h} and entry 55 uses {close, return_1h, return_24h, rsi_14, sma_20, volume_zscore_24h}. The substitution of return_168h for shorter returns and removal of macd_hist represents threshold/factor substitution rather than novel structural logic.
